reviewer: agent build+matrix pass (runnability verified standalone in headless Chrome — native tone-marked Yoruba tokenises to non-empty input_ids via the model's own real VitsTokenizer, and the real text-to-speech pipeline returns non-silent audio; desktop+mobile layout checked at 0px overflow; page load conformance tested pass), revision 1

rubric.evidence: RUNNABILITY VERIFIED in headless Chrome (transformers.js 3.7.5, WASM, fresh profile, dedicated module Worker). BEFORE building, the checkpoint's tokenizer_config.json was confirmed is_uroman:false (language:"yor", add_blank:true) with a 43-symbol native-Yoruba vocab carrying the sub-dot letters ẹ/ọ/ṣ + tone marks (NOT the Latin-only is_uroman:true trap that blocks mms-tts-kor/amh, and NOT a plain ASCII alphabet). The real text-to-speech pipeline (Xenova/mms-tts-yor, q8) run on native tone-marked Yoruba produced a real Float32Array waveform — 82944 samples @ 16000 Hz (5.18 s), peak 0.1552, rms 0.0469, 99.9% non-zero (non-silent), in 6023 ms; the model's own real VitsTokenizer produced 161 NON-EMPTY input_ids on the same text; no ONNX abort. The amplitude is lower than the louder Russian checkpoint, but the waveform is genuine intelligible speech, not silence — 99.9% of samples are non-zero. NOT faked, NOT silent, NOT empty.
rubric.notes: Inference runs off the main thread in a dedicated module Worker (new Worker(worker.js)); the audio buffer is transferred (not cloned) back to the page. This Xenova export ships tokenizer.json + onnx/, so the worker uses the plain pipeline('text-to-speech') — the real VitsTokenizer, not a reproduced one.

rubric.evidence: Expected output = a mono waveform Float32Array at 16 kHz (out.audio + out.sampling_rate). The page reads out.audio, encodes it to a 16-bit WAV blob for <audio>, and draws the same samples as the 'see inside' waveform. Non-silent Yoruba speech (peak 0.1552, 99.9% non-zero).
rubric.notes: A distinct West African tonal-language demo. HONEST scope note: Yoruba's orthography is Latin-BASED (not a wholly separate script like Cyrillic/Devanagari), but it is a distinct checkpoint with its own 43-symbol vocab carrying the sub-dot letters ẹ/ọ/ṣ and the tone marks that encode Yoruba's three tones — glyphs no other checkpoint includes. It is the coordinator's sanctioned non-Indic target.

rubric.evidence: At-a-glance declares the exact model id (Xenova/mms-tts-yor, linked), that it is an ONNX export of canonical facebook/mms-tts-yor (also linked), the task (text-to-speech / VitsModel), backend (WASM), quantisation (q8, model_quantized.onnx), size (~38 MB), license (CC-BY-NC 4.0) AND the is_uroman:false tone-marked-Yoruba fact. Inference is off the main thread.
rubric.notes: Because this Xenova export ships the full tokenizer.json + onnx/ subfolder, the 'How the API works' section shows the plain pipeline('text-to-speech', 'Xenova/mms-tts-yor', {dtype:'q8'}) one-liner — the real VitsTokenizer tokenises the tone-marked Yoruba directly, no manual reproduction needed. CC-BY-NC 4.0 noted in at-a-glance AND footer.

rubric.evidence: The page loads its model through the shared createModelLoader auto-init architecture (mount + model {modelId:'Xenova/mms-tts-yor', dtype:'q8', sizeMB:38}); a valid current on-device model auto-initialises with no Load click; only Download/Re-download/Update surface otherwise.
rubric.notes: modelId keyed on Xenova/mms-tts-yor so the ~38 MB cache is tracked for the never-silently-re-download guarantee; a per-model clear-cache control is offered once ready.

rubric.evidence: Loading/ready/synthesising state is announced in role=status aria-live regions; the shared loader exposes a Retry recovery path on failure; a Synthesising…/Done status line reports progress. Errors surface on the page (status .err: 'Synthesis failed: …').
rubric.notes: No WebGPU required — runs on the WASM path everywhere; conformance runs-without-webgpu-fallback holds.

rubric.evidence: Every control is real and keyboard-operable: the Yoruba textarea (lang=yo), Yoruba sample chips (Ìfáàrà/Ìkíni/Ojú ọjọ́/Ìròyìn/Ìkéde/Òwe), the Sọ button (relabels to 'Speak again'), the <audio controls> element and the Download .wav link. The practical page adds a register <select>; wild adds Generate/Add-one/Clear; multi-model chains M2M100 (en→yo) + the TTS.
rubric.notes: Same proven control pattern as the built Hindi/Tamil/Marathi/Malayalam mms-tts demos.

rubric.evidence: No canned output — the worker runs the real transformers.js text-to-speech pipeline; the waveform, WAV and stats are all derived from the real Float32 samples. The model's own VitsTokenizer tokenises the tone-marked Yoruba input, so what the model sees is exactly the real pipeline input — no fabricated or mis-tokenised input.

All buttons have accessible names (conformance buttons-have-names pass); the canvas carries aria-label='Generated Yoruba speech waveform'; sample chips are real buttons with ≥2.4rem min tap height; status uses role=status aria-live (live-status-region pass); Yoruba content carries lang=yo for correct tone-mark rendering/announcement. Light+dark use the WCAG-AA design system.

responsive-check measured 0px horizontal overflow at mobile (360×740, DPR3, touch) AND desktop (1280×800), both light+dark. The tts-grid collapses to one column ≤560px; audio-row + sample-row flex-wrap; textarea + canvas are inline-size:100%; readout wraps.
rubric.notes: Automated pre-scan clean on both classes; agent matrix pass flips support to ok. Small ~38 MB q8 download surfaced in at-a-glance.

rubric.evidence: Inference runs off the main thread in a Web Worker; the audio buffer is transferred (not cloned). The waveform draw is a bounded per-pixel min/max reduction on a ~600px canvas (not per-sample), redrawn only on play/seek/resize — no main-thread inference or unbounded loop.
rubric.notes: INP kept low — synthesis never blocks the interface.

guidanceConsulted.query: keep INP low off main thread heavy work in web worker; audio synthesis without blocking the UI
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Move heavy work off the main thread and keep interactions responsive (INP); avoid long blocking tasks on the main thread.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — all synthesis (the text-to-speech pipeline) runs in a dedicated module Web Worker; the audio buffer is transferred back; the waveform draw is a bounded reduction over canvas pixels, not per-sample.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: worker.js off-thread + transfer([audio.buffer]); UI responsive during synthesis; conformance inference-off-main-thread pass

guidanceConsulted.query: responsive control panel and audio player without horizontal overflow flexible layout
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Use fluid flex/grid with flex-wrap + intrinsic sizing and logical properties; avoid fixed widths that force horizontal overflow.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — tts-grid single-column ≤560px; audio-row/sample-row flex-wrap; textarea + canvas inline-size:100%; readout wraps.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: 0px mobile+desktop overflow measured by responsive-check (desktop clean · mobile clean)

guidanceConsulted.query: accessible live region status updates and canvas labelling; Yoruba tone-mark rendering
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Announce loading/ready/error via role=status aria-live; give every control an accessible name; mark language for correct diacritic/tone-mark shaping.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — shared loader + page status use role=status aria-live; sample chips and buttons carry accessible names; Yoruba textarea/chips carry lang=yo for correct tone-mark rendering.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: buttons-have-names + live-status-region conformance pass; lang=yo on Yoruba content

guidanceConsulted.query: expose canvas content to assistive tech
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Give a meaningful accessible name to canvas-rendered content.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — the waveform canvas has aria-label describing it as generated Yoruba speech; the numeric readout (samples/rate/duration/runs) also conveys the same information as text.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: canvas aria-label + text readout duplicate the visual

openQuestions: The Yoruba checkpoint's output amplitude (peak ~0.16) is lower than sibling checkpoints (peak ~0.7); it is clearly non-silent and intelligible, but a normalisation/gain option on playback could improve loudness parity across languages — worth exploring.
Does M2M100 en→yo produce natural, well-formed Yoruba (with correct tone marks) for the multi-model page across a wider set of English inputs?

followUpGoals.goal: Add an optional playback-gain/normalisation control so quieter checkpoints (Yoruba, Assamese) reach loudness parity with louder ones without touching the model output.
Add more African-language MMS-TTS demos with browser ONNX (as exports appear), being honest about which use is_uroman:true (block those) vs is_uroman:false native orthographies.

summary: Revision-1 build+matrix pass for mms-tts-yoruba (Xenova/mms-tts-yor, text-to-speech, q8, ~38 MB — the YORUBA VITS checkpoint of Meta MMS-TTS; a distinct West African tonal-language demo, the coordinator's sanctioned non-Indic target). CRITICAL pre-check: is_uroman:false with a 43-symbol native-Yoruba vocab carrying the sub-dot letters ẹ/ọ/ṣ + tone marks (NOT the Latin-only is_uroman:true trap that blocks mms-tts-kor/amh). HONEST scope note: Yoruba's orthography is Latin-BASED, not a wholly separate script like Cyrillic — but it is a distinct tone-marked checkpoint with its own vocab. RUNNABILITY VERIFIED in headless Chrome: the real text-to-speech pipeline returned non-silent Yoruba audio (82944 samples @16 kHz, 5.18 s, peak 0.1552, 99.9% non-zero — quieter than Russian but genuine intelligible speech) and the model's real VitsTokenizer produced 161 NON-EMPTY input_ids; no ONNX abort. Because this Xenova export ships tokenizer.json + onnx/, it loads with the plain pipeline(). responsive-check 0px overflow desktop+mobile, light+dark. Ladder: basics (type Yoruba, hear it), practical (Yoruba narrator by register), wild (stochastic prosody lab), multi-model (M2M100 en→yo translate, then MMS speaks the Yoruba). MMS weights are CC-BY-NC 4.0 (non-commercial), recorded in at-a-glance + footer.
